result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: 2s - 1 + n/2

JUSTIFICATION: The De Giorgi exponent (critical scaling exponent) for a dissipative PDE with diffusion order s in dimension n is derived from the scaling transformation u_lambda(x, t) = lambda^alpha u(lambda x, lambda^{2s} t). For the nonlinearity to scale consistently with the dissipation, the exponent is alpha = 2s - 1 + n/2.

Verification Table:
| PDE | Diffusion Order (s) | Dimension (n) | De Giorgi Exponent (2s - 1 + n/2) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 3D Navier-Stokes | 1 | 3 | 2(1) - 1 + 3/2 = 2.5 |
| 2D Navier-Stokes | 1 | 2 | 2(1) - 1 + 2/2 = 2.0 |
| 1D Burgers | 1 | 1 | 2(1) - 1 + 1/2 = 1.5 |
| SQG (Surface Quasi-Geostrophic) | 1/2 | 2 | 2(1/2) - 1 + 2/2 = 1.0 |
| MHD (Magnetohydrodynamics) | 1 | 3 | 2(1) - 1 + 3/2 = 2.5 |
| Fractional Navier-Stokes | s | n | 2s - 1 + n/2 |
